objects.title: Two-Block Homophily
objects.paper_statement:
The block size and edge intensities satisfy
\[
m\ge 2
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
a>b>0.
\]

objects.title: Balanced Sign Symmetry
objects.paper_statement:
The assignment law is sign-symmetric: for every \(z\in\{-1,1\}^n\),
\[
P(Z=z)=P(Z=-z).
\]

objects.title: Low-Scale Two-Block Regime
objects.paper_statement:
The two-block edge intensities satisfy
\[
a+3b<2m.
\]

objects.title: Odd Community Size
objects.paper_statement: The block size \(m\) is odd.

objects.title: Two-Block Graph Weights \(W_{ij}\)
objects.paper_statement:
The vertex set is the disjoint union \(A_m\cup B_m\), where
\[
|A_m|=|B_m|=m.
\]
The graph weights \(W_{ij}\) are given as follows: if \(i\ne j\) and \(i\) and \(j\) lie in the same block, then
\[
W_{ij}=\frac{a}{m};
\]
if \(i\) and \(j\) lie in opposite blocks, then
\[
W_{ij}=\frac{b}{m};
\]
and for every vertex \(i\),
\[
W_{ii}=0.
\]

objects.title: Block Elliptope \(\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\)
objects.paper_statement:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily}, the block elliptope is
\[
\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}
=
\left\{
X(u,v):
1-u\ge 0,\ 
1+(m-1)u-mv\ge 0,\ 
1+(m-1)u+mv\ge 0
\right\}.
\]

objects.title: Balanced sign class $\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{bal}}$
objects.paper_statement:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:balanced-sign-design}, the balanced sign class is
\[
\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{bal}}
=
\left\{
P \text{ on } \{-1,1\}^n :
P(Z=z)=P(Z=-z)\ \text{for every } z\in\{-1,1\}^n
\right\}.
\]

objects.title: Block-exchangeable class $\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{sym}}$
objects.paper_statement:
The block-exchangeable class is the set of all assignment laws
\[
P\in \mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{bal}}
\]
that are invariant under permutations within $A_m$, invariant under permutations within $B_m$, and invariant under swapping the two equal communities.

objects.title: Implementable covariance class $\mathcal C_m^{\pm}$
Design objective $F_{r,\kappa}$
objects.paper_statement:
For $r,\kappa\ge 0$, the design objective evaluated at a covariance matrix $X$ is
\[
F_{r,\kappa}(X)
=
\operatorname{tr}(L_m X)
+
r\,\operatorname{tr}(L_m^\dagger X)
+
\kappa \|X\|_{S_2}
+
\operatorname{tr}(J_n X).
\]

objects.title: Implementability loss $\Delta_m^{\pm}(r,\kappa)$
objects.paper_statement:
The implementability loss is
\[
\Delta_m^{\pm}(r,\kappa)
=
\inf_{P\in \mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{sym}}}
F_{r,\kappa}(X(P))
-
\inf_{X\in \mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}}
F_{r,\kappa}(X).
\]

objects.title: Block-sum law $\mathcal L_P(S_A,S_B)$
objects.paper_statement:
For an assignment law $P$, the block-sum law is the law under $P$ of
\[
(S_A,S_B)
=
\left(
\sum_{i\in A_m} Z_i,\,
\sum_{i\in B_m} Z_i
\right).
\]

objects.title: Symmetry Reduction Principle
objects.paper_statement:
Assume Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily}. Let \(r,\kappa\in[0,\infty)\). Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Relaxed matrices.)} For every positive semidefinite matrix \(X\in\mathbb R^{n\times n}\) with \(X_{ii}=1\) for every \(i\), let \(u\) and \(v\) be the averages of the off-diagonal within-community and across-community entries of \(X\), respectively. The block matrix \(X(u,v)\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:block-elliptope} has feasible block-elliptope parameters, and
\[
F_{r,\kappa}\bigl(X(u,v)\bigr)\le F_{r,\kappa}(X),
\]
where \(F_{r,\kappa}\) is Definition~\ref{obj:def:design-objective}.
\item \textbf{(Assignment laws.)} For every assignment law \(P\in\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{bal}}\) in the sense of Definition~\ref{obj:def:balanced-design-class}, its orbit average over the full two-block automorphism group belongs to \(\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{sym}}\) in the sense of Definition~\ref{obj:def:block-exchangeable-design-class}, has zero one-point margins,
\[
\mathbb E[Z_i]=0\qquad\text{for every }i,
\]
and satisfies
\[
F_{r,\kappa}\bigl(X(P^{\mathrm{orb}})\bigr)\le F_{r,\kappa}\bigl(X(P)\bigr).
\]
\item \textbf{(Relaxed infimum.)}
\[
\inf_{\substack{X\succeq0\\ X_{ii}=1\ \forall i}} F_{r,\kappa}(X)
=
\inf_{X\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}} F_{r,\kappa}(X).
\]
\item \textbf{(Implementable infimum.)}
\[
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{bal}}} F_{r,\kappa}\bigl(X(P)\bigr)
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{sym}}} F_{r,\kappa}\bigl(X(P)\bigr).
\]
\end{itemize}

objects.title: Cut-Corner Exactness
objects.paper_statement:
Assume the two-block homophily condition in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily}. Let \(r,\kappa\in\mathbb R\) satisfy
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Nonnegative tradeoff.)} \(0\le r\).
\item \textbf{(Nonnegative robustness.)} \(0\le \kappa\).
\item \textbf{(Cut region.)}
\[
r< r_{\mathrm{cut}}(m,a,b,\kappa)
:= \max\!\left\{0,\,
\min\!\left(
2b(a+b)\left(1-\frac{\kappa}{a-b}\right),
2b(2m-2b-\kappa)
\right)\right\}.
\]
\end{itemize}
Then \(X_{\mathrm{cut}}\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\), and for every
\(X\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\) with \(X\ne X_{\mathrm{cut}}\),
\[
F_{r,\kappa}(X_{\mathrm{cut}})<F_{r,\kappa}(X),
\]
where \(F_{r,\kappa}\) is the objective in Definition~\ref{obj:def:design-objective}. Moreover,
\[
\Delta_m^{\pm}(r,\kappa)=0,
\]
with \(\Delta_m^{\pm}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:implementability-gap}. Finally,
\(X_{\mathrm{cut}}\in\mathcal C_m^{\pm}\), and for every
\(X\in\mathcal C_m^{\pm}\) with \(X\ne X_{\mathrm{cut}}\),
\[
F_{r,\kappa}(X_{\mathrm{cut}})<F_{r,\kappa}(X),
\]
where \(\mathcal C_m^{\pm}\) is the class in Definition~\ref{obj:def:implementable-covariance-class}; the cut design satisfies
\[
P_{\mathrm{cut}}\in\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{sym}}
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
X(P_{\mathrm{cut}})=X_{\mathrm{cut}},
\]
with \(\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{sym}}\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:block-exchangeable-design-class}.

objects.title: Robust Corner Exactness
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(m\in\mathbb N\) and \(a,b,r\in\mathbb R\). Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Two-block homophily.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily} holds for \((m,a,b)\).
\item \textbf{(Nonnegative pseudoinverse weight.)} \(r\ge 0\).
\end{itemize}
For the objective \(F_{r,\kappa}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:design-objective}, the following statements hold.

First, there exists a finite nonnegative robustness weight \(\kappa\) such that \(I_n\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\) and
\[
F_{r,\kappa}(I_n)\le F_{r,\kappa}(X)
\qquad\text{for every }X\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}},
\]
if and only if
\[
a+3b=2m
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
r=2b(a+b).
\]

Second, on this affine-balanced locus, every positive robustness weight makes \(I_n\) the unique relaxed minimizer: if
\[
a+3b=2m
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
r=2b(a+b),
\]
then for every \(\kappa>0\),
\[
I_n\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}
\]
and, for every \(X\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\) with \(X\ne I_n\),
\[
F_{r,\kappa}(I_n)<F_{r,\kappa}(X).
\]

Third, the iid design satisfies
\[
P_{\mathrm{iid}}\in\mathcal P_m^{\mathrm{sym}}
\]
for the block-exchangeable class of Definition~\ref{obj:def:block-exchangeable-design-class}, and
\[
X(P_{\mathrm{iid}})=I_n.
\]

Finally, off the affine-balanced locus, \(I_n\) is never a minimizer at any positive finite robustness weight, and relaxed minimizers converge entrywise to \(I_n\) as \(\kappa\to\infty\). Precisely, if
\[
\neg\bigl(a+3b=2m\ \text{and}\ r=2b(a+b)\bigr),
\]
then for every \(\kappa>0\) it is not the case that
\[
F_{r,\kappa}(I_n)\le F_{r,\kappa}(X)
\qquad\text{for every }X\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}.
\]
Moreover, for any family \((X_\kappa)_{\kappa\in\mathbb R}\) such that, for every \(\kappa>0\),
\[
X_\kappa\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}
\quad\text{and}\quad
F_{r,\kappa}(X_\kappa)\le F_{r,\kappa}(X)
\ \text{ for every }X\in\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}},
\]
each matrix entry converges to the corresponding entry of \(I_n\):
\[
(X_\kappa)_{ij}\longrightarrow (I_n)_{ij}
\qquad\text{as }\kappa\to\infty
\]
for every \(i,j\in\{1,\ldots,n\}\).

objects.title: Positive-Gap Window
objects.paper_statement:
Suppose Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily} and Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:low-scale-two-block} hold for the two-block model with block size \(m\) and weights \(a,b\). Let
\[
q=2(m-1),\qquad
\kappa_{\mathrm{gap}}(m,a,b)
=
\frac{(2m-a-3b)(a-b)\sqrt q}{a+b},
\]
and
\[
r_{\mathrm{cut,gap}}(m,a,b,\kappa)
=
\max\left\{0,\;2b(a+b)\left(1-\frac{\kappa}{a-b}\right)\right\}.
\]
Define
\[
R_x^-(m,a,b,\kappa)
=
2b(a+b)\left(1+\frac{\kappa}{(a-b)\sqrt q}\right),
\qquad
R_x^+(m,a,b,\kappa)
=
(a+b)\left(2m-a-b-\frac{\kappa}{\sqrt q}\right),
\]
and
\[
r_{\mathrm{gap}}^-(m,a,b,\kappa)
=
\frac{R_x^-(m,a,b,\kappa)+R_x^+(m,a,b,\kappa)}{2},
\qquad
r_{\mathrm{gap}}^+(m,a,b,\kappa)
=
R_x^+(m,a,b,\kappa).
\]

Then the following two statements hold.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Odd block size.)} If Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:odd-community-size} holds, then for every \(\kappa\) satisfying
\[
0\le \kappa < \kappa_{\mathrm{gap}}(m,a,b),
\]
one has
\[
r_{\mathrm{cut,gap}}(m,a,b,\kappa)
<
r_{\mathrm{gap}}^-(m,a,b,\kappa)
<
r_{\mathrm{gap}}^+(m,a,b,\kappa).
\]
Moreover, for every
\[
r_{\mathrm{gap}}^-(m,a,b,\kappa)
<
r
<
r_{\mathrm{gap}}^+(m,a,b,\kappa),
\]
the spread covariance \(X_{\mathrm{spread}}\) belongs to \(\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\), and it is the unique strict minimizer of the design objective \(F_{r,\kappa}\) from Definition~\ref{obj:def:design-objective} over \(\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\): for every \(X\in \mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\) with \(X\ne X_{\mathrm{spread}}\),
\[
F_{r,\kappa}(X_{\mathrm{spread}})<F_{r,\kappa}(X).
\]
At the same time,
\[
X_{\mathrm{spread}}\notin \mathcal C_m^{\pm},
\]
where \(\mathcal C_m^{\pm}\) is the implementable covariance class of Definition~\ref{obj:def:implementable-covariance-class}, and the implementability loss of Definition~\ref{obj:def:implementability-gap} satisfies
\[
\Delta_m^{\pm}(r,\kappa)>0.
\]

\item \textbf{(Even block size.)} If \(m\) is even, then every covariance in the block elliptope is implementable:
\[
\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}\subseteq \mathcal C_m^{\pm}.
\]
\end{itemize}

objects.title: Sharp Rounding Loss
objects.paper_statement:
Under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily}, fix $r,\kappa\in[0,\infty)$.  Let
\[
q=2(m-1),\qquad
c_x=q\Bigl((a+b)+\frac{r}{a+b}\Bigr),\qquad
c_y=2b+\frac{r}{2b},\qquad
c_z=2m,
\]
let
\[
T_m=\{(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}):x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge0,\ qx+y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}=2m\},
\]
let
\[
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})
=c_xx+c_yy+c_zz_{\mathrm{sp}}
+\kappa\sqrt{qx^2+y^2+z_{\mathrm{sp}}^2},
\]
and let $d_m=0$ if $m$ is even and $d_m=2/m$ if $m$ is odd.  Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sharp equality and range.)}
\[
\rho_\star(m,a,b,r,\kappa)=\Delta_m^{\pm}(r,\kappa),
\qquad
\rho_\star(m,a,b,r,\kappa)\ge0,
\qquad
\Delta_m^{\pm}(r,\kappa)\ge0,
\]
where $\Delta_m^{\pm}$ is the implementability gap of Definition~\ref{obj:def:implementability-gap}.

\item \textbf{(Zero loss.)}
\[
\rho_\star(m,a,b,r,\kappa)=0
\]
if and only if there exists $(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m$ such that
\[
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})
\le
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x',y',z'_{\mathrm{sp}})
\quad\text{for every }(x',y',z'_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m,
\]
and
\[
d_m\le y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Strict robustness.)}
If $\kappa>0$, then there is a unique relaxed minimizer of $\phi_{r,\kappa}$ over $T_m$.  Moreover,
\[
\rho_\star(m,a,b,r,\kappa)=0
\]
if and only if every relaxed minimizer $(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m$ satisfies
\[
d_m\le y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}.
\]

\item \textbf{(Linear case.)}
If $\kappa=0$, then for every $(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m$, the point $(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})$ minimizes $\phi_{r,0}$ over $T_m$ if and only if
\[
x\ne0\Rightarrow \frac{c_x}{q}\le c_y\ \text{and}\ \frac{c_x}{q}\le c_z,
\]
\[
y\ne0\Rightarrow c_y\le \frac{c_x}{q}\ \text{and}\ c_y\le c_z,
\]
and
\[
z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ne0\Rightarrow c_z\le \frac{c_x}{q}\ \text{and}\ c_z\le c_y.
\]

\item \textbf{(Active-set formula.)}
If $\kappa>0$, then there exist an active support $S\subseteq\{x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}\}$ and a multiplier $\lambda\in\mathbb R$ such that $S$ and $\lambda$ are admissible for
\[
\alpha^\sharp=\Bigl(\frac{c_x}{q},c_y,c_z\Bigr),
\qquad
\beta^\sharp=\Bigl(\frac1q,1,1\Bigr),
\qquad
\kappa.
\]
Writing $t^{S,\lambda}=(t_x^{S,\lambda},t_y^{S,\lambda},t_z^{S,\lambda})$ for the associated active-set point on the simplex of mass $2m$, the reduced point
\[
\Bigl(\frac{t_x^{S,\lambda}}{q},t_y^{S,\lambda},t_z^{S,\lambda}\Bigr)
\]
belongs to $T_m$, minimizes $\phi_{r,\kappa}$ over $T_m$, and the relaxed reduced value is
\[
2m\,\lambda.
\]

\item \textbf{(Truncation correction.)}
If no relaxed minimizer $(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m$ satisfies $d_m\le y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}$, then the implementable reduced value equals the weighted-simplex objective with coefficients $\alpha^\sharp$, weights $\beta^\sharp$, and robustness weight $\kappa$, evaluated at the point on the truncation segment
\[
H_{d_m}=\{(2m-d_m,s,d_m-s):0\le s\le d_m\}
\]
selected by $\operatorname{truncSelector}(2m,d_m,\alpha^\sharp,\beta^\sharp,\kappa)$:
\[
\operatorname{val}_{\mathrm{impl}}(m,a,b,r,\kappa)
=
\Phi_{\alpha^\sharp,\beta^\sharp,\kappa}
\!\left(
2m-d_m,\,
s_\star,\,
d_m-s_\star
\right),
\]
where
\[
s_\star=\operatorname{truncSelector}(2m,d_m,\alpha^\sharp,\beta^\sharp,\kappa).
\]

\item \textbf{(Even block size.)}
If $m$ is even, then
\[
\rho_\star(m,a,b,r,\kappa)=0.
\]
\end{itemize}

objects.title: Block Spectral Coordinates
objects.paper_statement:
For any real numbers $r,\kappa,u,v$, assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Homophily.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily} holds for the block size $m$ and weights $a,b$.
\item \textbf{(Spectral coordinates.)} Set
\[
q=2(m-1),\qquad
x=1-u,\qquad
y=1+(m-1)u-mv,\qquad
z_{\mathrm{sp}}=1+(m-1)u+mv .
\]
\item \textbf{(Reduced coefficients.)} Set
\[
c_x=q\left((a+b)+\frac{r}{a+b}\right),\qquad
c_y=2b+\frac{r}{2b},\qquad
c_z=2m .
\]
\end{itemize}
Then the block covariance matrix $X(u,v)$ belongs to the block elliptope $\mathcal E_m^{\mathrm{blk}}$ if and only if
\[
x\ge 0,\qquad y\ge 0,\qquad z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge 0,\qquad
qx+y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}=2m .
\]
Moreover, for the design objective of Definition~\ref{obj:def:design-objective},
\[
F_{r,\kappa}\bigl(X(u,v)\bigr)
=
c_xx+c_yy+c_zz_{\mathrm{sp}}
+\kappa\sqrt{qx^2+y^2+z_{\mathrm{sp}}^2}.
\]
Finally,
\[
X_{\mathrm{cut}}=X(1,-1)
\quad\text{and}\quad
(1-1,\;1+(m-1)\cdot 1-m(-1),\;1+(m-1)\cdot 1+m(-1))
=(0,2m,0),
\]
while
\[
I_n=X(0,0)
\quad\text{and}\quad
(1-0,\;1+(m-1)\cdot 0-m\cdot 0,\;1+(m-1)\cdot 0+m\cdot 0)
=(1,1,1).
\]

objects.title: Cut Vertex Certificate
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(m\) be fixed and write \(q=2(m-1)\). Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive spectral multiplicity.)} \(q>0\).
\item \textbf{(Nonnegative robustness.)} \(\kappa\ge 0\).
\item \textbf{(Cut-side coefficient separation.)} The coefficients satisfy
\[
\frac{c_x}{q}>c_y+\kappa
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
c_z>c_y+\kappa .
\]
\end{itemize}
Then \((0,2m,0)\in T_m\), where
\[
T_m=\{(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}): x\ge 0,\ y\ge 0,\ z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge 0,\ qx+y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}=2m\}.
\]
Moreover, for every \((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m\) with
\((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\neq (0,2m,0)\),
\[
c_x\cdot 0+c_y(2m)+c_z\cdot 0
+\kappa\sqrt{q\cdot 0^2+(2m)^2+0^2}
<
c_xx+c_yy+c_zz_{\mathrm{sp}}
+\kappa\sqrt{qx^2+y^2+z_{\mathrm{sp}}^2}.
\]
Thus \((0,2m,0)\) is the unique minimizer on \(T_m\) of the reduced objective.

objects.title: Spread Vertex Certificate
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(m\in\mathbb N\), let \(c_x,c_y,c_z,\kappa\in\mathbb R\), and set \(q=2(m-1)\). Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive multiplicity.)} \(q>0\).
\item \textbf{(Nonnegative robustness.)} \(\kappa\ge 0\).
\item \textbf{(The \(y\)-coefficient is large.)} \(c_y>c_x/q+\kappa/\sqrt q\).
\item \textbf{(The \(z_{\mathrm{sp}}\)-coefficient is large.)} \(c_z>c_x/q+\kappa/\sqrt q\).
\end{itemize}
Define
\[
T_m=\{(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}):x\ge0,\ y\ge0,\ z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge0,\ qx+y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}=2m\}
\]
and
\[
\phi(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})
=
c_xx+c_yy+c_zz_{\mathrm{sp}}
+\kappa\sqrt{qx^2+y^2+z_{\mathrm{sp}}^2}.
\]
Then \((2m/q,0,0)\in T_m\), and for every \((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m\) with
\((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\neq (2m/q,0,0)\),
\[
\phi(2m/q,0,0)<\phi(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}).
\]

objects.title: Frobenius Center Certificate
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(m\in\mathbb N\), set \(q=2(m-1)\), and suppose \(q>0\).  For any real coefficients \(c_x,c_y,c_z\) and any real \(\kappa\), define
\[
T_m=\{(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}): x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge 0,\ qx+y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}=2m\}
\]
and
\[
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})
=
c_xx+c_yy+c_zz_{\mathrm{sp}}
+\kappa\sqrt{q x^2+y^2+z_{\mathrm{sp}}^2}.
\]
Then:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Frobenius center.)} The point \((1,1,1)\) belongs to \(T_m\), and for every \((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m\) with \((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\ne(1,1,1)\),
\[
\sqrt{q\cdot 1^2+1^2+1^2}
<
\sqrt{q x^2+y^2+z_{\mathrm{sp}}^2}.
\]
\item \textbf{(Certificate necessity.)} If \((1,1,1)\) minimizes \(\phi_{r,\kappa}\) over \(T_m\), that is, if
\[
\phi_{r,\kappa}(1,1,1)\le
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})
\quad\text{for all }(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m,
\]
then
\[
\frac{c_x}{q}=c_y
\quad\text{and}\quad
c_y=c_z.
\]
\item \textbf{(Strict optimality.)} If \(\kappa>0\), \(\frac{c_x}{q}=c_y\), and \(c_y=c_z\), then for every \((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m\) with \((x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\ne(1,1,1)\),
\[
\phi_{r,\kappa}(1,1,1)
<
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}).
\]
\end{itemize}

objects.title: Parity-Truncated Reduced Slice
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(m\ge 2\) be an integer and let \(u,v\in\mathbb R\). Write
\[
x=1-u,\qquad
y=1+(m-1)u-mv,\qquad
z_{\mathrm{sp}}=1+(m-1)u+mv,
\]
and let \(d_m=0\) when \(m\) is even and \(d_m=2/m\) when \(m\) is odd. Then the block covariance matrix \(X(u,v)\) belongs to the implementable covariance class \(\mathcal C_m^{\pm}\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:implementable-covariance-class} if and only if
\[
x\ge 0,\qquad y\ge 0,\qquad z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge 0,\qquad
2(m-1)x+y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}=2m,
\]
and
\[
y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge d_m .
\]

objects.title: Weighted Simplex Active Set
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(m\ge 2\), let \(M>0\) satisfy \(M=2m\), and let
\(\mathcal I=\{x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}\}\). Let
\(\alpha=(\alpha_i)_{i\in\mathcal I}\), let \(\kappa\ge 0\), and let
\(\beta=(\beta_i)_{i\in\mathcal I}\) have strictly positive entries with
\[
  \beta_x=\frac{1}{2(m-1)},\qquad
  \beta_y=\beta_{z_{\mathrm{sp}}}=1 .
\]
For \(t\in\mathbb R^{\mathcal I}\), write
\[
  \Delta_M=\Bigl\{t:\ t_i\ge 0\ \text{for all }i\in\mathcal I,\ 
  \sum_{i\in\mathcal I}t_i=M\Bigr\},
\]
and
\[
  \Phi(t)=\sum_{i\in\mathcal I}\alpha_i t_i
  +\kappa\sqrt{\sum_{i\in\mathcal I}\beta_i t_i^2}.
\]
Then the following hold.
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive robustness.)} If \(\kappa>0\), there is a unique pair
\((S,\lambda)\), where \(S\subseteq\mathcal I\) is nonempty and
\(\lambda\in\mathbb R\), such that
\[
  \sum_{i\in S}\frac{(\lambda-\alpha_i)^2}{\beta_i}=\kappa^2,\qquad
  \alpha_i<\lambda\ \text{for every }i\in S,\qquad
  \lambda\le \alpha_j\ \text{for every }j\notin S .
\]
For this unique pair, define
\[
  t_i^\star=
  \begin{cases}
  \displaystyle
  M\,\frac{(\lambda-\alpha_i)/\beta_i}
  {\sum_{h\in S}(\lambda-\alpha_h)/\beta_h}, & i\in S,\\[1.1em]
  0, & i\notin S .
  \end{cases}
\]
Then \(t^\star\in\Delta_M\), and \(t^\star\) is the unique minimizer of
\(\Phi\) over \(\Delta_M\) in the strict sense that, for every
\(s\in\Delta_M\) with \(s\ne t^\star\),
\[
  \Phi(t^\star)<\Phi(s).
\]
Its value is
\[
  \Phi(t^\star)=M\lambda .
\]

\item \textbf{(Zero robustness.)} If \(\kappa=0\), then for every
\(t\in\mathbb R^{\mathcal I}\),
\[
  \Bigl(t\in\Delta_M\ \text{and}\ 
  \Phi(t)\le \Phi(s)\ \text{for every }s\in\Delta_M\Bigr)
  \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
  t\in\Delta_M\ \text{and whenever }t_i\ne 0,\ 
  \alpha_i\le \alpha_j\ \text{for every }j\in\mathcal I .
\]
Equivalently, the minimizer set is exactly the exposed
\(\alpha\)-minimizing face of \(\Delta_M\).
\end{itemize}

objects.title: Weighted Simplex Truncation
objects.paper_statement:
Let \(m\ge 2\), let \(M,d\in\mathbb R\), and let \(\alpha=(\alpha_x,\alpha_y,\alpha_z)\), \(\beta=(\beta_x,\beta_y,\beta_z)\), and \(\kappa\in\mathbb R\). Suppose:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Mass and truncation.)} \(M>0\), \(M=2m\), and \(0\le d\le M\).
\item \textbf{(Weights.)} Each \(\beta_i\) is strictly positive and
\[
\beta=\left(\frac{1}{2(m-1)},1,1\right),
\]
so in particular \(\beta_y=\beta_z=1\).
\item \textbf{(Robustness.)} \(\kappa\ge 0\).
\end{itemize}
Write
\[
K_d=\{t\in\Delta_M:t_y+t_z\ge d\},
\qquad
\Phi(t)=\sum_i\alpha_i t_i+\kappa\sqrt{\sum_i\beta_i t_i^2}.
\]
For \(\kappa>0\), for every nonempty active support \(S\subseteq\{x,y,z\}\) and multiplier \(\lambda\) satisfying
\[
\sum_{i\in S}\frac{(\lambda-\alpha_i)^2}{\beta_i}=\kappa^2,\qquad
\alpha_i<\lambda\ \text{for }i\in S,\qquad
\lambda\le \alpha_j\ \text{for }j\notin S,
\]
let \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\) be the active-set point
\[
t^{\mathrm{rel}}_i=
\begin{cases}
\displaystyle
\frac{M((\lambda-\alpha_i)/\beta_i)}
{\sum_{h\in S}(\lambda-\alpha_h)/\beta_h}, & i\in S,\\[1em]
0, & i\notin S .
\end{cases}
\]
If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\in K_d\), then
\[
\Phi(t^{\mathrm{rel}})\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d.
\]
If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}_y+t^{\mathrm{rel}}_z<d\), then
\[
t^\star=(M-d,s^\star,d-s^\star)\in K_d
\]
and
\[
\Phi(t^\star)\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d,
\]
where \(s^\star=\operatorname{truncSelector}(M,d,\alpha,\beta,\kappa)\), namely with
\[
\delta=\alpha_y-\alpha_z,\qquad A=\beta_x(M-d)^2,
\]
\[
s^\star=
\begin{cases}
0, & \displaystyle \delta\ge \frac{\kappa d}{\sqrt{A+d^2}},\\[0.8em]
d, & \displaystyle \delta\le -\frac{\kappa d}{\sqrt{A+d^2}},\\[0.8em]
\displaystyle
\frac{d-\delta\sqrt{(A+d^2/2)/(\kappa^2-\delta^2/2)}}{2},
& \text{otherwise.}
\end{cases}
\]

For \(\kappa=0\), let \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\) be a point of the exposed \(\alpha\)-minimizing face
\[
\{t\in\Delta_M:\ t_i\ne 0\Rightarrow \alpha_i\le \alpha_j\ \text{for every }j\}.
\]
Assume the tie convention that, if this exposed face contains at least one point of \(K_d\), then the selected point \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\) lies in \(K_d\). If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}\in K_d\), then
\[
\Phi(t^{\mathrm{rel}})\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d.
\]
If \(t^{\mathrm{rel}}_y+t^{\mathrm{rel}}_z<d\), then
\[
t^\star=(M-d,s^\star,d-s^\star)\in K_d
\]
and
\[
\Phi(t^\star)\le \Phi(s)
\qquad\text{for every }s\in K_d,
\]
where \(s^\star=\operatorname{truncSelector}(M,d,\alpha,\beta,0)\), equivalently, in the ordered selector convention, \(s^\star=0\) if \(\alpha_y-\alpha_z\ge 0\) and \(s^\star=d\) if \(\alpha_y-\alpha_z<0\).

objects.title: Reduced Gap Identity
objects.paper_statement:
For any real $r$ and any $\kappa\ge 0$, under Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:two-block-homophily}, the implementability loss of Definition~\ref{obj:def:implementability-gap} satisfies
\[
\Delta_m^{\pm}(r,\kappa)
=
\inf_{\substack{(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m\\ y+z_{\mathrm{sp}}\ge d_m}}
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})
-
\inf_{(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}})\in T_m}
\phi_{r,\kappa}(x,y,z_{\mathrm{sp}}).
\]

dfaac2568905456d.label: TwoBlockHomophily
dfaac2568905456d.statement:
/-- Two-block homophily: `m ≥ 2` and `a > b > 0` (within-block weight strictly
dominates the positive cross-block weight). -/
def TwoBlockHomophily (m : ℕ) (a b : ℝ) : Prop :=
  2 ≤ m ∧
  b < a ∧            -- @realizes a(range: 0 < b < a pins a ∈ (0,∞))
  0 < b              -- @realizes b(range: 0 < b pins b ∈ (0,∞))

ea107410423baff7.label: BalancedSignDesign

7e4a43a3c987a038.label: LowScaleTwoBlock
7e4a43a3c987a038.statement:
/-- Low-scale two-block normalization `a + 3b < 2m` (ensures `D = 2m − a − 3b > 0`). -/
def LowScaleTwoBlock (m : ℕ) (a b : ℝ) : Prop := a + 3 * b < 2 * (m : ℝ)

e3d6d00fa871c7c3.label: OddCommunitySize
e3d6d00fa871c7c3.statement:
/-- Odd community size (parity feasibility: forces `S_A, S_B` odd, so `y + z ≥ 2/m`). -/
def OddCommunitySize (m : ℕ) : Prop := Odd m

0f23bd66cefb22ff.label: twoBlockGraph

eff39ecccf314bb6.label: BlockElliptopeMem
eff39ecccf314bb6.statement:
/-- Membership witness for the block-symmetric elliptope slice `E_m^blk`, over the
two-block homophily regime. Its member atom is `ass:two-block-homophily`; the three
inequalities are exactly the PSD constraints of the spectral coordinates
`x = 1−u`, `y = 1+(m−1)u−mv`, `z = 1+(m−1)u+mv`. -/
structure BlockElliptopeMem (m : ℕ) (a b u v : ℝ) : Prop where
  /-- Member atom: the two-block homophily regime. -/
  homophily : TwoBlockHomophily m a b
  /-- `x = 1 − u ≥ 0`. -/
  psd_x : 0 ≤ 1 - u
  /-- `y = 1 + (m−1)u − m v ≥ 0`. -/
  psd_y : 0 ≤ 1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u - (m : ℝ) * v
  /-- `z = 1 + (m−1)u + m v ≥ 0`. -/
  psd_z : 0 ≤ 1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u + (m : ℝ) * v

ae600bd04914cb1b.label: BalancedDesignClass
ae600bd04914cb1b.statement:
/-- The balanced design class `P_m^bal`, bundling the single sign-symmetry atom.
@realizes P_m^bal(designs with global sign symmetry)
@realizes P(cluster member: the bundled law `D : FiniteDesign (Fin (2*m) → Bool)`, a
probability law on {-1,1}^n via the carrier's `p_nonneg`/`p_sum` fields) -/
structure BalancedDesignClass (m : ℕ) (D : FiniteDesign (Fin (2 * m) → Bool)) : Prop where
  /-- Member atom: global sign symmetry. -/
  balanced : BalancedSignDesign m D

bdd322fd9baa3c26.label: blockExchangeableDesignClass

abe1f9a0eae52b0f.label: implementableCovarianceClass

4c6f6c51229733a0.label: designObjective
4c6f6c51229733a0.statement:
/-- The normalized design objective
`F_{r,κ}(X) = Tr(L_m X) + r Tr(L_m^† X) + κ ‖X‖_{S_2} + Tr(J_n X)`.
@realizes F_{r,kappa}(X)(Tr(L_m X) + r Tr(L_m^† X) + κ‖X‖ + Tr(J_n X))
@realizes r(carrier ℝ; homophily tradeoff weight on the `r * Tr(L^† X)` term)
@realizes kappa(carrier ℝ; robustness weight on the Schatten-2 penalty `κ‖X‖_{S_2}`; range
[0,∞) pinned by the `0 ≤ kappa` premises threaded through the consuming theorems/lemmas) -/
noncomputable def designObjective (m : ℕ) (a b r kappa : ℝ)
    (X : Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ) : ℝ :=
  Matrix.trace (twoBlockLaplacian m a b * X)
    + r * Matrix.trace (twoBlockLaplacianPinv m a b * X)
    + kappa * frobeniusNorm X
    + Matrix.trace (allOnesMatrix m * X)

0f75b1ba18a03432.label: implementabilityGap
0f75b1ba18a03432.statement:
/-- The implementability gap `Δ_m^±(r,κ) = inf_{P ∈ P_m^sym} F(X(P)) − inf_{X ∈ E_m^blk} F(X)`,
as an `sInf` difference over the objective images.
@realizes Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)(implementable inf − relaxed inf; CARRIER of the gap. The bare
`sInf − sInf` real difference is a plain `ℝ`; its core-declared range `[0,∞)` is NOT carried
by this `def` alone but by the CONJUNCTION of this carrier with the companion range lemma
`implementabilityGap_nonneg` in `Helpers/GapReduction.lean`, which pins `0 ≤ Δ_m^±` via
the reduced constrained-minus-unconstrained identity.)
@realizes P(cluster member — AUTHORITATIVE link into the `sharp_rho_star` target block:
the implementable `sInf` is taken over `designObjective … '' implementableCovarianceClass m`,
i.e. over the second moments `X(P)` of the probability laws `P ∈ P_m^sym`; this is the
decl through which the P realization cluster enters `thm:sharp-rho-star`)
@realizes kappa(carrier ℝ; robustness argument of the gap; range [0,∞) pinned by the
`0 ≤ kappa` premises of the consuming theorems `sharp_rho_star`/`rounding_gap_reduction`) -/
noncomputable def implementabilityGap (m : ℕ) (a b r kappa : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  sInf (designObjective m a b r kappa '' implementableCovarianceClass m)
    - sInf (designObjective m a b r kappa '' blockElliptope m a b)

58c89e56c5bc633c.label: blockSumHandle
58c89e56c5bc633c.statement:
/-- The block-sum law handle `B(P)`: the joint pmf of the two community sums
`(S_A, S_B)` under `P` (the OEQ parity handle).
@realizes B(P)(joint law of the two community sums) -/
noncomputable def blockSumHandle (m : ℕ) (D : FiniteDesign (Fin (2 * m) → Bool))
    (kl : ℤ × ℤ) : ℝ :=
  D.E (fun z => if blockSumA m z = kl.1 ∧ blockSumB m z = kl.2 then 1 else 0)

6a6c7618350cbf7d.label: symmetry_reduction
6a6c7618350cbf7d.statement:
/-- Symmetry reduction. The orbit-average over the full two-block automorphism group
(permutations within `A_m`, within `B_m`, and the community swap) is the *explicit,
named* no-worse witness — not merely some existing block-symmetric point (the note's
load-bearing clauses):

* every PSD unit-diagonal matrix `X` is mapped by orbit averaging to the block-symmetric
  elliptope point `blockSymMatrix m (uOf m X) (vOf m X) ∈ E_m^blk`, whose block entries
  `uOf m X`, `vOf m X` are the averaged within- and across-community entries of `X`, with
  weakly smaller objective, and
* every balanced law `D ∈ P_m^bal` is mapped by the same orbit averaging to the
  block-exchangeable law `orbitAvgDesign m D ∈ P_m^sym`, with the same zero one-point
  margins and weakly smaller objective.

The two `sInf` equalities (relaxed = block-symmetric slice, implementable = symmetry
reduced) are the consequences. (`0 ≤ r` and `0 ≤ κ` are the definitional-domain
regularity premises, reflected in the assumption table.) -/
lemma symmetry_reduction (m : ℕ) (a b r kappa : ℝ)
    (hHom : TwoBlockHomophily m a b) (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hk : 0 ≤ kappa) :
    (∀ X : Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ, X.PosSemidef → (∀ i, X i i = 1) →
      BlockElliptopeMem m a b (uOf m X) (vOf m X) ∧
        designObjective m a b r kappa (blockSymMatrix m (uOf m X) (vOf m X))
          ≤ designObjective m a b r kappa X) ∧
    (∀ D : FiniteDesign (Fin (2 * m) → Bool), BalancedDesignClass m D →
        orbitAvgDesign m D ∈ blockExchangeableDesignClass m ∧
        (∀ i, (orbitAvgDesign m D).E (fun z => signOf m z i) = 0) ∧
        designObjective m a b r kappa (assignmentSecondMoment m (orbitAvgDesign m D))
          ≤ designObjective m a b r kappa (assignmentSecondMoment m D)) ∧
    sInf (designObjective m a b r kappa ''
        { X : Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ | X.PosSemidef ∧ ∀ i, X i i = 1 })
      = sInf (designObjective m a b r kappa '' blockElliptope m a b) ∧
    sInf ((fun D => designObjective m a b r kappa (assignmentSecondMoment m D)) ''
        { D | BalancedDesignClass m D })
      = sInf ((fun D => designObjective m a b r kappa (assignmentSecondMoment m D)) ''
        blockExchangeableDesignClass m)

d3848e4ce87ff194.label: cut_corner_exactness
d3848e4ce87ff194.statement:
/-- **Cut-corner exactness.** Under two-block homophily, for `0 ≤ r < r_cut(m,a,b,κ)`
the cut covariance `X_cut` is the unique minimizer of `F_{r,κ}` over `E_m^blk`; the
implementability gap is zero; `X_cut` is the unique minimizer over `C_m^pm`; and the
cut design `P_cut ∈ P_m^sym` realizes it with `X(P_cut) = X_cut`.
(The `ass:balanced-sign-design` atom is realized through membership in the
block-exchangeable class `C_m^pm`/`P_m^sym`, which bundles `BalancedDesignClass`.) -/
theorem cut_corner_exactness (m : ℕ) (a b r kappa : ℝ)
    (hHom : TwoBlockHomophily m a b)
    (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) -- @realizes r(range 0 ≤ r pins r ∈ [0,∞))
    (hk0 : 0 ≤ kappa) -- @realizes kappa(range 0 ≤ κ pins κ ∈ [0,∞), its definitional domain)
    (hr : r < rCut m a b kappa) : -- @realizes r_cut(m,a,b,kappa)(cut region r < r_cut)
    (cutCovariance m ∈ blockElliptope m a b ∧
      ∀ X ∈ blockElliptope m a b, X ≠ cutCovariance m →
        designObjective m a b r kappa (cutCovariance m) < designObjective m a b r kappa X) ∧
    implementabilityGap m a b r kappa = 0 ∧
    (cutCovariance m ∈ implementableCovarianceClass m ∧
      ∀ X ∈ implementableCovarianceClass m, X ≠ cutCovariance m →
        designObjective m a b r kappa (cutCovariance m) < designObjective m a b r kappa X) ∧
    cutDesign m ∈ blockExchangeableDesignClass m ∧
    assignmentSecondMoment m (cutDesign m) = cutCovariance m

726d252b625be966.label: robust_corner_exactness
726d252b625be966.statement:
/-- **Robust-corner exactness.** Under two-block homophily: `I_n` is a minimizer of
`F_{r,κ}` over `E_m^blk` for some finite `κ` iff `a+3b=2m` and `r=2b(a+b)`; on this
locus, for every `κ > 0`, `I_n` is the unique relaxed minimizer and `P_iid ∈ P_m^sym`
attains it with `X(P_iid)=I_n`; off the locus `I_n` is never a finite-`κ` minimizer,
though relaxed minimizers converge (entrywise) to `I_n` as `κ → ∞`.

The iid-exactness frontier `kappa_iid(m,a,b,r)` — the smallest robustness weight from
which `I_n = X(P_iid)` is optimal — has no closed-form decl (bound only as a symbol,
like `r_star`). Its [0,∞) space with `kappa_iid ≥ kappa_cut` is now pinned by the
dedicated carrier predicate `IsIidExactnessFrontier` in `Basic.lean` (the `0 ≤ κ_iid`
and `κ_cut ≤ κ_iid` conjuncts fix the space and ordering, the optimality clause its
frontier role). This theorem supplies the frontier's EXACT finiteness criterion, not its
value: the two tagged clauses below give a finite such weight EXISTS iff the
affine-balanced locus `a+3b=2m ∧ r=2b(a+b)` holds (off-locus `kappa_iid = +∞`), and on
the locus every `κ > 0` is admissible. -/
theorem robust_corner_exactness (m : ℕ) (a b r : ℝ) (hHom : TwoBlockHomophily m a b)
    (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) : -- @realizes r(range 0 ≤ r pins r ∈ [0,∞))
    ((∃ kappa : ℝ, 0 ≤ kappa ∧
        (1 : Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ) ∈ blockElliptope m a b ∧
        ∀ X ∈ blockElliptope m a b,
          designObjective m a b r kappa 1 ≤ designObjective m a b r kappa X)
      -- @realizes kappa_iid(m,a,b,r)(finiteness criterion: a finite iid-exactness
      -- weight EXISTS iff the affine-balanced locus a+3b=2m ∧ r=2b(a+b); off-locus =+∞)
      ↔ (a + 3 * b = 2 * (m : ℝ) ∧ r = 2 * b * (a + b))) ∧
    ((a + 3 * b = 2 * (m : ℝ) ∧ r = 2 * b * (a + b)) →
      -- @realizes kappa_iid(m,a,b,r)(on-locus value: every κ>0 makes I_n the unique
      -- relaxed minimizer, so the infimal admissible weight kappa_iid = 0 (≥ kappa_cut))
      ∀ kappa : ℝ, 0 < kappa →
        ((1 : Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ) ∈ blockElliptope m a b ∧
          ∀ X ∈ blockElliptope m a b, X ≠ 1 →
            designObjective m a b r kappa 1 < designObjective m a b r kappa X)) ∧
    (iidDesign m ∈ blockExchangeableDesignClass m ∧
      assignmentSecondMoment m (iidDesign m) = 1) ∧
    (¬ (a + 3 * b = 2 * (m : ℝ) ∧ r = 2 * b * (a + b)) →
      (∀ kappa : ℝ, 0 < kappa →
        ¬ (∀ X ∈ blockElliptope m a b,
            designObjective m a b r kappa 1 ≤ designObjective m a b r kappa X)) ∧
      (∀ (Xseq : ℝ → Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ),
        (∀ kappa : ℝ, 0 < kappa → Xseq kappa ∈ blockElliptope m a b ∧
          ∀ X ∈ blockElliptope m a b,
            designObjective m a b r kappa (Xseq kappa) ≤ designObjective m a b r kappa X) →
        ∀ i j, Tendsto (fun kappa => Xseq kappa i j) atTop
          (𝓝 ((1 : Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ) i j))))

d2779b86e38f300c.label: gap_window
d2779b86e38f300c.statement:
/-- **Positive-gap window.** Under two-block homophily and low-scale: *if* `m` is odd,
then for every `0 ≤ κ < κ_gap` the frontiers are strictly ordered
`r_cut_gap < r_gap^- < r_gap^+`, and for `r ∈ (r_gap^-, r_gap^+)` the spread vertex
is the unique relaxed minimizer of `F` over `E_m^blk` but is not ±1 implementable, so
`Δ_m^±(r,κ) > 0`. The oddness is necessary as a *separate* even-`m` case (NOT nested
under the odd hypothesis, so it does not fire vacuously): for even `m` the whole block
elliptope slice is implementable, hence the claimed positive gap is false. -/
theorem gap_window (m : ℕ) (a b : ℝ)
    (hHom : TwoBlockHomophily m a b) (hLow : LowScaleTwoBlock m a b) :
    (OddCommunitySize m → ∀ kappa : ℝ, 0 ≤ kappa → kappa < kappaGap m a b →
      rCutGap a b kappa < rGapMinus m a b kappa ∧ -- @realizes r_cut(m,a,b,kappa)
      rGapMinus m a b kappa < rGapPlus m a b kappa ∧
      -- @realizes r(bound within the gap window rGapMinus < r < rGapPlus ⊂ [0,∞))
      ∀ r : ℝ, rGapMinus m a b kappa < r → r < rGapPlus m a b kappa →
        (spreadCovariance m ∈ blockElliptope m a b ∧
          ∀ X ∈ blockElliptope m a b, X ≠ spreadCovariance m →
            designObjective m a b r kappa (spreadCovariance m)
              < designObjective m a b r kappa X) ∧
        spreadCovariance m ∉ implementableCovarianceClass m ∧
        0 < implementabilityGap m a b r kappa) ∧
    (Even m → blockElliptope m a b ⊆ implementableCovarianceClass m)

878b6f6cfc03ae45.label: sharp_rho_star
878b6f6cfc03ae45.statement:
theorem sharp_rho_star (m : ℕ) (a b r kappa : ℝ) (hHom : TwoBlockHomophily m a b)
    (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) -- @realizes r(range 0 ≤ r pins r ∈ [0,∞))
    (hk : 0 ≤ kappa) : -- @realizes kappa(range 0 ≤ κ pins κ ∈ [0,∞), its definitional domain)
    roundingLossCertificate m a b r kappa = implementabilityGap m a b r kappa ∧
    -- @realizes rho_star(m,a,b,r,kappa)(standing range clause 0 ≤ ρ_⋆ pinning space [0,∞);
    -- discharged by the companion range lemma `roundingLossCertificate_nonneg` in Basic)
    0 ≤ roundingLossCertificate m a b r kappa ∧
    -- @realizes Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)(standing range clause 0 ≤ Δ_m^± pinning space [0,∞);
    -- discharged by the companion range lemma `implementabilityGap_nonneg` in Basic)
    0 ≤ implementabilityGap m a b r kappa ∧
    (roundingLossCertificate m a b r kappa = 0 ↔
      ∃ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z ∧
        (∀ x' y' z', InReducedTriangle m x' y' z' →
          reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x y z
            ≤ reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x' y' z') ∧
        parityThreshold m ≤ y + z) ∧
    (0 < kappa →
      (∃! t : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ, InReducedTriangle m t.1 t.2.1 t.2.2 ∧
        ∀ x' y' z', InReducedTriangle m x' y' z' →
          reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa t.1 t.2.1 t.2.2
            ≤ reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x' y' z') ∧
      (roundingLossCertificate m a b r kappa = 0 ↔
        ∀ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z →
          (∀ x' y' z', InReducedTriangle m x' y' z' →
            reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x y z
              ≤ reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x' y' z') →
          parityThreshold m ≤ y + z)) ∧
    (kappa = 0 →
      ∀ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z →
        ((∀ x' y' z', InReducedTriangle m x' y' z' →
            reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x y z
              ≤ reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x' y' z')
          ↔ (x ≠ 0 → cX m a b r / qParam m ≤ cY b r ∧ cX m a b r / qParam m ≤ cZ m) ∧
            (y ≠ 0 → cY b r ≤ cX m a b r / qParam m ∧ cY b r ≤ cZ m) ∧
            (z ≠ 0 → cZ m ≤ cX m a b r / qParam m ∧ cZ m ≤ cY b r))) ∧
    -- Active-set computation of the relaxed minimizer (lem:weighted-simplex-active-set):
    -- for κ > 0 the unique relaxed argmin over `T_m` is the active-set point of an
    -- admissible support/multiplier `(S, λ)` under the change of variables `t = (q x, y, z)`
    -- (`α = sharpAlpha`, `β = sharpBeta`), with relaxed value `M · λ` (`M = 2m`).
    (0 < kappa →
      ∃ S : Finset (Fin 3), ∃ lam : ℝ,
        IsAdmissibleSupport (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) kappa S lam ∧
        InReducedTriangle m
          (activeSetPoint (2 * (m : ℝ)) (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) S lam 0 / qParam m)
          (activeSetPoint (2 * (m : ℝ)) (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) S lam 1)
          (activeSetPoint (2 * (m : ℝ)) (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) S lam 2) ∧
        (∀ x' y' z', InReducedTriangle m x' y' z' →
          reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa
              (activeSetPoint (2 * (m : ℝ)) (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) S lam 0 / qParam m)
              (activeSetPoint (2 * (m : ℝ)) (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) S lam 1)
              (activeSetPoint (2 * (m : ℝ)) (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) S lam 2)
            ≤ reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x' y' z') ∧
        relaxedReducedValue m a b r kappa = 2 * (m : ℝ) * lam) ∧
    -- Truncation correction (lem:weighted-simplex-truncation): when the relaxed argmin
    -- set is disjoint from the implementable slice `{y+z ≥ d_m}`, the implementable value
    -- is attained on the truncation segment `{y+z = d_m}` at the endpoint/interior selector
    -- `truncSelector`, so `Δ_m^±` is computed by this boundary-segment formula.
    ((¬ ∃ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z ∧
        (∀ x' y' z', InReducedTriangle m x' y' z' →
          reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x y z
            ≤ reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa x' y' z') ∧
        parityThreshold m ≤ y + z) →
      implementableReducedValue m a b r kappa =
        wsObj (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) kappa
          (truncSegPoint (2 * (m : ℝ)) (parityThreshold m)
            (truncSelector (2 * (m : ℝ)) (parityThreshold m)
              (sharpAlpha m a b r) (sharpBeta m) kappa))) ∧
    (Even m → roundingLossCertificate m a b r kappa = 0)

37e768f5d4271ea6.label: block_spectral_coordinates
37e768f5d4271ea6.statement:
/-- `X(u,v)` lies in `E_m^blk` iff its spectral coordinates lie in the reduced
triangle `T_m`, and the design objective equals the reduced form `φ` with
`c_x = q((a+b)+r/(a+b))`, `c_y = 2b + r/(2b)`, `c_z = 2m` and `q = 2(m−1)`.
The last two conjuncts record the corner coordinate readings the note states:
`X_cut = X(1,−1)` has spectral coordinates `(0, 2m, 0)` and `I_n = X(0,0)` has
spectral coordinates `(1, 1, 1)`. -/
lemma block_spectral_coordinates (m : ℕ) (a b r kappa u v : ℝ)
    (hHom : TwoBlockHomophily m a b) :
    (blockSymMatrix m u v ∈ blockElliptope m a b ↔
      InReducedTriangle m (1 - u) (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u - (m : ℝ) * v)
        (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u + (m : ℝ) * v)) ∧
    designObjective m a b r kappa (blockSymMatrix m u v)
      = reducedObjective (qParam m) (cX m a b r) (cY b r) (cZ m) kappa
          (1 - u) (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u - (m : ℝ) * v)
          (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u + (m : ℝ) * v) ∧
    (cutCovariance m = blockSymMatrix m 1 (-1) ∧
      ((1 : ℝ) - 1, 1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * 1 - (m : ℝ) * (-1),
          1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * 1 + (m : ℝ) * (-1))
        = ((0 : ℝ), 2 * (m : ℝ), (0 : ℝ))) ∧
    ((1 : Matrix (Fin (2 * m)) (Fin (2 * m)) ℝ) = blockSymMatrix m 0 0 ∧
      ((1 : ℝ) - 0, 1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * 0 - (m : ℝ) * 0,
          1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * 0 + (m : ℝ) * 0)
        = ((1 : ℝ), (1 : ℝ), (1 : ℝ)))

f193c6dd43aa47ae.label: cut_vertex_certificate
f193c6dd43aa47ae.statement:
/-- Cut-vertex certificate: on `T_m`, if `κ ≥ 0`, `c_x/q > c_y + κ` and `c_z > c_y + κ`
then `(0, 2m, 0)` is the unique minimizer of `φ`. -/
lemma cut_vertex_certificate (m : ℕ) (cx cy cz kappa : ℝ)
    (hq : 0 < qParam m) (hk : 0 ≤ kappa)
    (h1 : cx / qParam m > cy + kappa) (h2 : cz > cy + kappa) :
    InReducedTriangle m 0 (2 * (m : ℝ)) 0 ∧
    ∀ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z → (x, y, z) ≠ (0, 2 * (m : ℝ), 0) →
      reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa 0 (2 * (m : ℝ)) 0
        < reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa x y z

2dd2f08445bf1fd8.label: spread_vertex_certificate
2dd2f08445bf1fd8.statement:
/-- Spread-vertex certificate: on `T_m`, if `κ ≥ 0`, `c_y > c_x/q + κ/√q` and
`c_z > c_x/q + κ/√q` then `(2m/q, 0, 0)` is the unique minimizer of `φ`. -/
lemma spread_vertex_certificate (m : ℕ) (cx cy cz kappa : ℝ)
    (hq : 0 < qParam m) (hk : 0 ≤ kappa)
    (h1 : cy > cx / qParam m + kappa / Real.sqrt (qParam m))
    (h2 : cz > cx / qParam m + kappa / Real.sqrt (qParam m)) :
    InReducedTriangle m (2 * (m : ℝ) / qParam m) 0 0 ∧
    ∀ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z → (x, y, z) ≠ (2 * (m : ℝ) / qParam m, 0, 0) →
      reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa (2 * (m : ℝ) / qParam m) 0 0
        < reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa x y z

bc935965a5aebc3c.label: frobenius_center_certificate
bc935965a5aebc3c.statement:
/-- Frobenius-center certificate: `(1,1,1)` uniquely minimizes the weighted
Frobenius term on `T_m`; it minimizes the full objective for finite `κ` only if
`c_x/q = c_y = c_z`, and under that equality with `κ > 0` it is the unique
minimizer. -/
lemma frobenius_center_certificate (m : ℕ) (cx cy cz kappa : ℝ) (hq : 0 < qParam m) :
    (InReducedTriangle m 1 1 1 ∧
      ∀ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z → (x, y, z) ≠ (1, 1, 1) →
        Real.sqrt (qParam m * 1 ^ 2 + 1 ^ 2 + 1 ^ 2)
          < Real.sqrt (qParam m * x ^ 2 + y ^ 2 + z ^ 2)) ∧
    ((∀ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z →
        reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa 1 1 1
          ≤ reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa x y z) →
      cx / qParam m = cy ∧ cy = cz) ∧
    (0 < kappa → cx / qParam m = cy → cy = cz →
      ∀ x y z, InReducedTriangle m x y z → (x, y, z) ≠ (1, 1, 1) →
        reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa 1 1 1
          < reducedObjective (qParam m) cx cy cz kappa x y z)

e98aecdca174717b.label: pm_reduced_slice_characterization
e98aecdca174717b.statement:
/-- The ±1 covariance image of the block-exchangeable class in spectral
coordinates is exactly the reduced triangle truncated by the parity threshold
`d_m` (`0` for even `m`, `2/m` for odd `m`). In particular, for odd `m` the spread
vertex `(m/(m−1), 0, 0)` is not implementable, while for even `m` the whole slice is.

Requires `2 ≤ m`: for `m ∈ {0,1}` the within-block parameter `u` does not appear in
`X(u,v)` (blocks have no within-block off-diagonal pair), so `X(u,v)` is independent
of `u` while the reduced coordinates are not, and the characterization fails. -/
lemma pm_reduced_slice_characterization (m : ℕ) (hm : 2 ≤ m) (u v : ℝ) :
    blockSymMatrix m u v ∈ implementableCovarianceClass m ↔
      (InReducedTriangle m (1 - u) (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u - (m : ℝ) * v)
          (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u + (m : ℝ) * v) ∧
        parityThreshold m ≤ (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u - (m : ℝ) * v)
          + (1 + ((m : ℝ) - 1) * u + (m : ℝ) * v))

e973e38ac7498dc0.label: rounding_gap_reduction
e973e38ac7498dc0.statement:
/-- The implementability gap equals the reduced-coordinate implementable-minus-relaxed
value: `Δ_m^±(r,κ) = implementableReducedValue − relaxedReducedValue`. -/
lemma rounding_gap_reduction (m : ℕ) (a b r kappa : ℝ)
    (hHom : TwoBlockHomophily m a b) (hk : 0 ≤ kappa) :
    implementabilityGap m a b r kappa
      = implementableReducedValue m a b r kappa - relaxedReducedValue m a b r kappa
